---
title: "Section 6: Implementation Plan"
parent: "ST04"
---

# Implementation Plan {#implementation}

## 6.1 Phase 1: BG/NBD with CLVTools (Deployed)

The production implementation uses CLVTools (CRAN Nov 2025):

```r
library(CLVTools)

# Step 1: Prepare transaction data
transactions <- df_sales_by_customer_by_date[, .(
  customer_id, date = as.Date(payment_time), price = total_spent
)]

# Step 2: Create CLVTools data object
clv_data <- clvdata(
  data.transactions = transactions,
  date.format = "ymd",
  time.unit = "week",
  estimation.split = NULL,  # use all data
  name.id = "customer_id",
  name.date = "date",
  name.price = "price"
)

# Step 3: Fit BG/NBD — estimates (r, alpha, a, b)
bgf <- bgnbd(clv.data = clv_data, verbose = TRUE)

# Step 4: Compute P(alive) and expected transactions
results <- predict(bgf, prediction.end = 12)  # 12-week forecast
# Returns: PAlive, CET (conditional expected transactions)

# Step 5: Map back to data_by_customer
data_by_customer[results, on = "customer_id", `:=`(
  p_alive = i.PAlive,
  nrec_prob = 1 - i.PAlive,  # backward compatible
  btyd_expected_transactions = i.CET
)]
```

## 6.2 Core Function: `analysis_btyd()`

**File**: `global_scripts/04_utils/fn_analysis_btyd.R`

```r
analysis_btyd <- function(data_by_customer,
                          df_sales_by_customer_by_date,
                          time_unit = "week",
                          prediction_periods = 12,
                          fallback_params = NULL,
                          verbose = TRUE) {
  # 1. Prepare transactions for CLVTools
  # 2. Create clvdata object
  # 3. Fit BG/NBD (with fallback_params if provided)
  # 4. Compute P(alive)
  # 5. Overwrite nrec_prob = 1 - P(alive)
  # 6. Return updated data_by_customer
}
```

Key design decisions:

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Package | CLVTools | Active maintenance (2025), modern S4 API, covariate support |
| Time unit | Week | Balances granularity with numerical stability |
| Fallback | Keep original nrec_prob | Graceful degradation — never crash pipeline |
| Integration point | After `analysis_dna()` | Overwrites nrec_prob, adds p_alive column |

## 6.3 Pipeline Integration: `fn_D01_02_core.R`

The BTYD call is inserted after `analysis_dna()` in the D01_02 pipeline:

```r
# Existing: run analysis_dna() per product_line slice
dna_results <- analysis_dna(...)

# NEW: Replace nrec_prob with BTYD P(alive)
tryCatch({
  dna_results$data_by_customer <- analysis_btyd(
    data_by_customer = dna_results$data_by_customer,
    df_sales_by_customer_by_date = by_date_subset,
    fallback_params = fallback_params_for_slice,
    verbose = TRUE
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  message(sprintf("[%s] BTYD failed (%s), keeping original nrec_prob",
                  platform_id, e$message))
})
```

## 6.4 Two-Pass Empirical Bayes Architecture

The key innovation beyond standard BTYD: **Two-Pass Empirical Bayes** for handling product line slices with insufficient data.

```
Pass 1: Independent MLE per product_line slice
  -> Collect (r, alpha, a, b) from each successful estimation
  -> Identify failed/degenerate slices

Pass 2: Re-run failed slices with median(successful params) as start values
  -> fallback_params = median of Pass 1 successes
  -> Provides informed priors for small/degenerate slices
```

See [Section 7](07_production_results.qmd) for actual Pass 1/Pass 2 results.

## 6.5 DRV Dependency Markers

```r
#####
# DERIVATION: D01_02 Customer DNA Analysis (with BTYD)
# GROUP: D01 (Customer DNA Analysis)
# CONSUMES: df_sales_by_customer, df_sales_by_customer_by_date
# PRODUCES: df_dna_by_customer (with p_alive, nrec_prob from BTYD)
# DEPENDS_ON_ETL: amz_ETL_sales_2TR
# PRINCIPLE: UX_P002, MP064, DEV_R038
#####
```

## 6.6 Future Phases

| Phase | Model | File | Status |
|-------|-------|------|--------|
| **Phase 1** | BG/NBD (CLVTools) | `fn_analysis_btyd.R` | **Deployed** (2026-03-03) |
| Phase 2 | Pareto/GGG (regularity) | TBD | Planned |
| Phase 3 | Covariate-enriched | TBD | Research |
